commands/commit.md.verdict: NO VIOLATION — all sections are concise functional command/security documentation; verbose_lines=0 after applying 'rules not stories' principle

summary.severity_threshold_applied: absolute: critical>=200 lines OR >=30%; major>=100 lines OR >=15%; minor>=50 lines. percentage applied alongside absolute for small files. No in-scope file exceeded any threshold.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex raised 3 issues: (1) scope drift — non-commands/agents .md files were over-classified as advisory; (2) severity rationale should cite both absolute and percentage thresholds; (3) commit.md verbose_lines accounting was misleading (labeled 'potentially verbose' for functional constraint docs). One OBSERVATION_ONLY finding: no missed critical verbosity in commands/commit.md.

findings.issue: Scope drift — scripts/README.md, scripts/INDEX.md, root INDEX.md, .py, .json, and test files are not command/agent prompt docs; treating scripts/README.md as ADVISORY ONLY is still too much scope
findings.proposed_fix: Move all non-commands/*.md / non-agents/*.md files to out_of_scope/skipped_files with reason: not command/agent markdown prompt documentation; generated inventory/data/source/test file
findings.rationale: Correct — prompt-inspector scope is command/agent .md files only per detection rules. All non-command/agent files moved to skipped_files[] with explicit out-of-scope reasons.

findings.issue: Severity rationale used only 'below 50 lines' without citing the percentage-based threshold that also applies, which could matter for small files with a high verbosity ratio
findings.proposed_fix: Replace summary with: No severity assigned to out-of-scope files. For in-scope findings, apply absolute verbose_lines and verbosity_percentage thresholds before classifying severity.
findings.rationale: Applied — summary.severity_threshold_applied now explicitly cites both absolute line-count and percentage thresholds.

findings.issue: commands/commit.md candidate accounting was misleading — the draft labeled ~28 lines as 'potentially verbose' when the Close-gate reference and Privilege guard note are functional security/authorization constraints
findings.proposed_fix: Set commands/commit.md verbose_lines=0; state: Reviewed Usage, Close-gate reference, and Privilege guard note; all are concise functional command/security documentation, no verbosity finding.
findings.rationale: Applied — verbose_lines set to 0 and all sections classified as functional in skipped_findings_detail. The sections contain mandatory security constraint rules, not explanatory narrative.

findings.issue: No missed critical verbosity found in the only in-scope file listed, commands/commit.md. The ## Usage flag table is concise CLI reference, not a verbose tutorial.
findings.rationale: Observation_only — confirms no false negatives on commands/commit.md. Logged for completeness.

codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: Applied 3 codex fixes: (1) all non-command/agent files reclassified as out-of-scope in skipped_files[]; (2) summary severity_threshold_applied now cites both absolute and percentage thresholds; (3) commands/commit.md verbose_lines set to 0 with all sections explicitly classified as functional.
